title: Increased Accountability for Nonconsensual Pornography Act of 2025
short_title: Increased Accountability for Nonconsensual Pornography Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes stronger laws to protect people when someone shares private intimate pictures or videos of them without asking first. The law now covers more types of private images and makes it clearer what counts as giving permission to share them. If someone breaks this law by sharing intimate images without permission, they can now be forced to pay much more money as punishment. The maximum amount they might have to pay goes up from one hundred fifty thousand dollars to five hundred thousand dollars. This helps protect people from having their private images shared when they do not want that to happen.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Increased Accountability for Nonconsensual Pornography Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Civil Action Relating to Disclosure of Intimate Images
sections.text: This section changes an existing law from twenty twenty two about lawsuits over sharing intimate pictures without permission. The changes include three main updates. First, when defining consent, the law now requires that a person must be both conscious and competent when they agree to something. Second, the law expands what counts as an intimate image to include any identifiable person engaging in sexually explicit conduct. Third, the maximum amount of money someone can be awarded in damages increases from one hundred fifty thousand dollars to five hundred thousand dollars.
